title: astragalus distortus var. engelmannii
intro: Astragalus distortus var. engelmannii, also known as Mormon tea, is a type of perennial legume native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. It is valued for its medicinal properties, which include antioxidant and anti-inflammatory compounds, as well as its ability to modulate the immune system. The health benefits of Mormon tea are attributed to its ability to reduce stress, improve respiratory function, and boost the immune system, making it a popular herbal remedy for conditions such as bronchitis and pneumonia. Mormon tea can be consumed as a tea, capsule, or tincture, and is often used in traditional medicine to promote overall health and well-being.
